select_when: A result looks dramatic, but causality is weak and the team may be explaining variance with a confident story too early.
Decision quality depends on anchoring to base rates, longer-run distributions, and repeated measurement instead of the loudest recent datapoint.
Rewards, punishments, or process changes occurred next to an extreme outcome, so the team may over-credit the intervention.
The real question is whether performance shifted structurally or merely deviated from the mean for a while.
avoid_when: There is evidence that the underlying system, market, or process quality has genuinely changed, so waiting for reversion would ignore a new mean.
Regression-to-the-mean is being used as a lazy wait-and-see excuse when the deteriorating trend actually requires intervention.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on variance discipline, outside-view anchoring, and resisting noisy causal stories rather than broad statistics instruction.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor-style analytics overlays because the raw source is already explicit on base-rate discipline and structural-change checks.
Did not add higher-order fields because this batch stops at the frozen Wave 1 to Wave 3 contracts.
open_questions: The first avoid-when condition is explicit in substance but still lightly compressed from the raw warning about true system change.

select_when.note: The raw strengths section is explicit about dramatic outcomes, weak causality, and baseline discipline; the final triggers mainly compress that surface into routing form.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions are directly supported by explicit warnings about real system change and the lazy wait-and-see trap.

input_type.note: The source activates on extreme deviations and noisy streaks, but the compact input label is reviewed operational normalization.

output_type.note: The raw model separates noise from structural change; the output label compresses that into an operational check.

reasoning_types.note: Regression to the mean clearly uses probabilistic and causal reasoning, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
